# THRIVE R — Reach and relationships

All powers install **Available Inactive**. A dashboard launcher opens a preview only; it never performs an external action.

## PWR-09 — Community Relationship & Trust-Building Planner

**Pillar:** R<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Develop reciprocal, non-extractive community relationships based on listening, shared value and transparent commitments.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** community-defined need; approved relationship history; partner roles; commitments; power/resource considerations; accessibility needs; owner<br>
**Method:** map stakeholders without individual dossiers; identify mutual value and burden; design listening and feedback-back; record commitments, compensation questions and stop rules<br>
**Output:** Relationship Plan, commitment ledger, listening agenda, burden review and partnership questions<br>
**Human checkpoint:** named community and organization representatives approve commitments<br>
**Prohibited:** no contact harvesting, covert influence, tokenization, individual profiling, outreach send, promise, donation, payment or partnership execution<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-10 — Aggregate Audience Insight & Ethical Segmentation Studio

**Pillar:** R<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Use approved aggregate research to understand needs and contexts without creating individual leads or sensitive profiles.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved aggregate dataset; purpose; lawful/ethical basis; definitions; minimum-cell rule; allowed segments; data owner; review date<br>
**Method:** validate provenance and denominators; use coarse need/context segments; test exclusion and stigma risks; compare non-targeted alternatives; define expiry and suppression<br>
**Output:** Audience Insight Brief, aggregate segment cards, bias/exclusion questions and decision queue<br>
**Human checkpoint:** data, privacy, equity and marketing owners approve any downstream use<br>
**Prohibited:** no individual lead/contact list, re-identification, health-condition inference, protected-class proxy, look-alike audience, eligibility or high-impact targeting<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-11 — Partnership, Referral-Network & Co-Marketing Governance

**Pillar:** R<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Prepare due diligence and a shared-value proposal for organizations without initiating referrals, contracts or co-marketing.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved partner category; public/approved organization facts; proposed shared purpose; roles; data flows; claims; conflicts; review routes<br>
**Method:** verify identity and authority; compare values and service fit; map clinical boundary, referral neutrality, data, brand, accessibility, economics and exit risks; draft questions and options<br>
**Output:** Partner Due-Diligence Brief, role/RACI proposal, risk register and decision queue<br>
**Human checkpoint:** legal, compliance, clinical, privacy, finance and executive humans approve formal arrangements<br>
**Prohibited:** no patient referral, endorsement, lead exchange, contract, signature, data sharing, payment, booking or public announcement<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.

## PWR-12 — Event, Outreach & Community Activation Planner

**Pillar:** R<br>
**Install state:** `Available Inactive`<br>
**Purpose:** Design an accessible, ethical event or outreach concept with clear handoffs and contingency planning.<br>
**Allowed inputs:** approved objective; public or aggregate audience context; venue/channel; capacity; partners; accessibility; claims; budget range; operations owner<br>
**Method:** define useful experience and non-goals; map roles, access, safety, consent, content, capacity and follow-up; include low-tech alternatives and stop conditions<br>
**Output:** Activation Plan, run-of-show draft, accessibility checklist, approved-content queue and after-action measures<br>
**Human checkpoint:** event, clinical, safety, legal, communications and operations owners authorize execution<br>
**Prohibited:** no invitation send, registration/booking, individual attendee list, health screening, purchase, contract, public post or real-event command<br>
**Finish state:** preview receipt with source, owner, version, unresolved questions, human decision, expiry and safe exit; no external or official-system action.
